newsSince: No material new company-specific news since June 9. The largest single order in company history (>EUR150m for eight reactor coolant pumps, Eastern Europe nuclear) was announced March 2026, and FY25 (>EUR3B order intake and revenue) and the ~EUR100m liquid-DC-cooling 2030 ambition both predate the window. KSB remains a quiet name with no fresh June headlines.

verdicts.rankRationale: Strict latent AI-node lens: liquid-cooling/water-reuse pump thesis is real and articulated (mgmt targets ~EUR100m DC-cooling revenue by 2030 from ~zero). No new June catalyst, but the franchise is intact and well-placed at rank 13; +5.9% pop is sentiment, not news. Hold.
verdicts.bull: Pumps/valves are direct beneficiaries of liquid-cooling and water-reuse buildout; >EUR3B orders, record nuclear order, and an explicit DC-cooling revenue ramp give a credible AI-node pathway.
verdicts.neutral: DC-cooling is a small future slice of a broadly industrial/nuclear/water order book; thesis is early.
verdicts.bear: AI-cooling revenue is still ~zero today; bulk of demand is legacy industrial/process pumps unrelated to AI.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Disclosed DC liquid-cooling design wins or revenue toward the EUR100m/2030 target.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Slow conversion of the cooling opportunity; industrial capex cyclicality.
